exact_rational.reason: DASHU-008 reproduces at 3136 bytes and larger; direct reproducers remain available outside the active corpus.

findings.title: Directed log2 differs from MPFR across magnitude ranges
findings.summary: Dashu log2 differs from MPFR both near 2^-24 under downward rounding and at f64::MIN_POSITIVE under upward rounding.
findings.impact: The error is many ULPs rather than a tie-breaking or signed-zero difference.
findings.deduplication: The direct and sequence inputs isolate the same log2 implementation; the discrepancies vary in size but share the operation-level root.

inputs.expected: operation=log2 reason=composed correctly rounded result differs from MPFR
operation=log2 reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

findings.title: Rational construction triggers internal allocation panic in GCD
findings.summary: Constructing and reducing a fuzzed rational reaches dashu-int's divide-and-conquer GCD and panics with `internal error: not enough memory allocated`.
findings.impact: Valid large integer inputs can panic during rational normalization.
findings.deduplication: A unique raw backend panic identified by its dashu-int memory.rs assertion and GCD stack.

inputs.expected: internal error: not enough memory allocated
internal error: not enough memory allocated

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Downward subtraction can step one extra f64 lower
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: Both near f64::MAX and near 2.0, Dashu-backed downward subtraction returns one representable value below MPFR's correctly rounded result.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The returned lower bound is unnecessarily loose by one representable value and is not correctly rounded.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: The two magnitude ranges share the same subtraction, direction, and one-extra-ULP symptom and are grouped as a normalization/rounding defect.

inputs.expected: operation=sub reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR
operation=sub reason=composed correctly rounded result differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Directed f32 exp underflow returns an incorrect zero or negative subnormal
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: At large negative finite f32 inputs, upward exp returns +0 instead of the minimum positive subnormal, while downward exp can return a negative minimum subnormal instead of +0.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The upward result is below the exact positive value, and the downward manifestation has an impossible negative sign for exp.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: The two manifestations are grouped as one f32 exponential-underflow/sign defect. The panic seen with Dashu 0.4 is not part of this 0.5 report.

inputs.expected: operation=exp reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR
operation=exp reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Downward expm1 reports overflow when f64::MAX is representable
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: For a large finite input under downward rounding, Dashu reports overflow while MPFR returns f64::MAX.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: A valid finite directed result is rejected as an error at the overflow boundary.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: Kept separate from the exp panic because the observable contract and failure mode differ.

inputs.expected: operation=expm1 reason=Dashu returned an error for a valid MPFR result

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Downward ln1p of the minimum subnormal rounds upward
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: Downward ln1p of f64::from_bits(1) returns the same minimum subnormal, while MPFR rounds the slightly smaller exact result to +0.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The purported lower bound is above the exact result.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: A standalone unary boundary bug with a distinct operation and output pattern.

inputs.expected: operation=ln1p reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Directed division reports overflow when the correctly rounded result is exactly +/-f64::MAX
resolved_by_adapter_fix.upstream_status: All three retained manifestations still reproduce with Dashu 0.5.0.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: Dividing 1 by a tiny positive f64 under downward rounding reports overflow while MPFR returns +f64::MAX, and dividing a value near -1 by a tiny positive subnormal under upward rounding reports overflow while MPFR returns the representable -f64::MAX.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: A valid finite directed result that lands exactly on the +/-f64::MAX boundary is rejected as an error rather than returned.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: The downward/+f64::MAX and upward/-f64::MAX binary manifestations and the composed-sequence report share one root cause: the overflow check fires at the f64::MAX magnitude boundary even though the correctly rounded directed result is representable. They are grouped as one division overflow-boundary defect independent of sign and rounding direction.

inputs.expected: operation=div reason=Dashu returned an error for a valid MPFR result
operation=div reason=Dashu returned an error for a valid MPFR result
operation=div reason=composed operation success/error behavior differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Downward negative power of f64::MAX returns a negative subnormal
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: For powi(f64::MAX, -53) under downward rounding, Dashu returns -f64::from_bits(1), while MPFR returns +0.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The result has the wrong sign and lies on the opposite side of zero from the positive exact value.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: A distinct powi sign/underflow defect, separate from ln1p's positive subnormal boundary error.

inputs.expected: operation=powi reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Upward addition near -1 skips one representable f64
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: Adding the minimum positive subnormal to the f64 immediately above -1 under upward rounding returns 0xbfeffffffffffffd instead of MPFR's 0xbfeffffffffffffe.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The result is one representable value too high and is not correctly rounded.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: A distinct addition normalization case; kept separate from subtraction near f64::MAX.

inputs.expected: operation=add reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR
operation=add reason=composed correctly rounded result differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Upward expm1 of the minimum normal f64 rounds downward
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: Upward expm1 of f64::MIN_POSITIVE returns the input unchanged, while MPFR returns the next larger f64.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The purported upper bound is below the exact result.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: Kept separate from the expm1 overflow error because this is a small-input rounding defect on the opposite boundary.

inputs.expected: operation=expm1 reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Downward ln near one is one ULP too low
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: For input 0x3ff0000000005500 under downward rounding, Dashu ln returns one f64 below the MPFR result.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The returned bound is unnecessarily low and not correctly rounded.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: The sequence inputs agree at the failing step, isolating a standalone ln discrepancy distinct from log2.

inputs.expected: operation=ln reason=composed correctly rounded result differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Directed big-integer-to-f64 conversion rounds toward larger magnitude regardless of direction
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: Converting -(2^128 - 1) to f64 under upward rounding returns -3.402823669209385e38 instead of MPFR's less-negative adjacent value, and converting +(2^128 - 1) as a natural under downward rounding returns 3.402823669209385e38 instead of MPFR's smaller adjacent value.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: Around the 2^128 magnitude, Dashu's directed integer/natural conversion always rounds to the larger-magnitude neighbor, violating the requested directed bound in both directions.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: The upward signed-integer and downward natural manifestations share the same integer conversion path and the same too-large-magnitude symptom on the same 2^128-1 magnitude, so they are grouped as one directed integer-conversion rounding defect. Kept separate from rational/nearest conversion behavior, which uses a different path and rounding mode.

inputs.expected: operation=integer_to_f64 reason=directed conversion differs from MPFR
operation=integer_to_f64 reason=directed conversion differs from MPFR
operation=natural_to_f64 reason=directed conversion differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.upstream_dashu: Genuine dashu bug: IBig::to_f64() reports Exact for an inexact value. The adapter defends by not trusting the Approximation tag, so it no longer manifests through opendp-num, but the dashu defect remains. Evidence: examples/root_cause.rs.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Upward directed division is one ULP too high
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: Dividing two finite f64 values near the normal/subnormal boundary under upward rounding returns one representable value above MPFR's correctly rounded result.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The result is a valid upper bound but is not the tight correctly-rounded value promised by the contract.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: Kept separate from DASHU-010 because that finding is an erroneous overflow result rather than an in-range one-ULP discrepancy.

inputs.expected: operation=div reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Adding -0 to a tiny positive f64 produces a vastly larger value
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: Under upward rounding, adding -0 to 0x0000003ff0020000 returns 0x3d80000000000000 instead of preserving the tiny positive operand.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The output is approximately 2^-39 rather than a subnormal-scale value, a large magnitude error in a basic directed addition.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: Kept separate from one-ULP addition findings because this result changes the exponent by hundreds of bins and specifically involves a signed-zero operand.

inputs.expected: operation=add reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.

resolved_by_adapter_fix.title: Directed multiply of two positive minimum subnormals returns the negative minimum subnormal
resolved_by_adapter_fix.summary: Multiplying f64::from_bits(1) by itself under downward rounding returns -f64::from_bits(1) (0x8000000000000001), while MPFR returns +0. The exact product 2^-2148 is positive and underflows to +0.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.impact: The product of two positive operands is returned with a negative sign, an impossible result that lies on the wrong side of zero; downstream directed lower bounds derived from it are invalid.
resolved_by_adapter_fix.deduplication: A standalone directed multiplication sign/underflow defect. Kept separate from the exp (DASHU-004) and powi (DASHU-011) underflow-sign findings because it is the multiply operation on a different backend path, and separate from the addition and division rounding findings because those preserve sign and differ by at most one representable value.

inputs.expected: operation=mul reason=correctly rounded value differs from MPFR

resolved_by_adapter_fix.resolution: Resolved in opendp-num by the exact-rational directed rounding in src/backend/dashu.rs (exact RBig arithmetic + single correctly-rounded directed conversion, native prechecks removed). No longer reproduces on the fixed adapter; see findings/ROOT_CAUSE.md.
